data.Name: Adaptive Fighting
data.Short Description: Once per day as a move action, gain the benefit of a combat feat you don’t have
data.Benefit: Select three combat feats that you do not have but whose prerequisites you meet. Once per day as a move action, you can gain the benefit of one of these feats for 1 minute. Each time you gain a level, you can replace one of these three selected feats with a different feat that you don’t have but meet the prerequisites for.

data.Name: Advanced Melee Weapon Proficiency
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with advanced melee weapons
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency with advanced melee weapons (see Weapon Proficiency on page 243).

data.Name: Amplified Glitch
data.Short Description: Disrupt devices, causing targets to become shaken for 1 round or more
data.Benefit: As a standard action, you can wirelessly introduce a fast-acting virus into nearby technological devices to briefly cause them to malfunction in loud, surprising ways. You can use this ability on one target creature per character level, no two of which can be more than 30 feet apart. Each target must either be carrying technological devices or be within sight and hearing of such devices. While the locations of such devices are up to the GM, most public spaces (aside from technologically underdeveloped areas) contain enough technology for this feat to function. Your glitch causes alarms to go off, automated devices to spin and flail, readouts to flash brightly, and so on. With a successful Computers check (DC = 15 + 1 per target + one and one half × the CR of the highest-CR target), all targets are shaken for 1 round, plus 1 additional round for every 5 by which your result exceeds the DC. Once you have targeted a creature with this feat, that foe is immune to this ability for 24 hours.

data.Name: Antagonize
data.Short Description: Anger a foe, causing it to become off-target and take a -2 penalty to skill checks for 1 round or more
data.Benefit: As a standard action, you can antagonize a foe that can see and hear you by attempting a Diplomacy or Intimidate check (DC = 10 + your opponent’s total Sense Motive skill bonus, or 15 + one and one half × the opponent’s CR, whichever is higher). If you succeed, the foe is off-target and takes a –2 penalty to all skill checks for 1 round plus 1 additional round for every 5 by which your result exceeds the DC, or until it makes an attack against you, forces you to attempt a saving throw, or damages you (whichever comes first). Once you have attempted to antagonize a foe, that foe is immune to this ability for 24 hours. This is a language-dependent ability.

data.Name: Barricade
data.Short Description: Create your own fragile cover
data.Benefit: As a move action, you can stack and reinforce objects that are too small or too fragile to provide cover into a single square of adjacent cover. The GM has discretion over whether enough such objects are nearby to allow you to use this ability, but most urban and wilderness settings not specifically described as empty or barren have enough such material to allow at least one such temporary barricade to be built. The barricade grants partial cover against attacks with line of effect that pass through it. If the barricade is in a square that already granted partial cover, it instead grants normal cover. For more information about cover, see page 253. The barricade is temporary and not particularly durable. When determining its hardness and Hit Points, treat it as a piece of equipment with an item level equal to half your total ranks in Engineering (minimum 1st level). Additionally, once it or a creature adjacent to it is hit by an attack, the barricade collapses at the beginning of your turn in 1d4 rounds (unless the barricade is destroyed completely by the attack). There isn’t normally enough material for you to build a second barricade in exactly the same space unless you are in a particularly crowded area (as determined by the GM).

data.Name: Basic Melee Weapon Proficiency
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with basic melee weapons
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency with basic melee weapons (see Weapon Proficiency on page 243).

data.Name: Blind-Fight
data.Short Description: Reroll miss chances from concealment
data.Benefit: In melee, every time you miss because of concealment (see page 253), you can reroll your miss chance percentile roll one time to see if you actually hit (see page 243). You aren’t flat-footed against melee attacks from creatures you can’t see, and you can withdraw from creatures you can’t perceive. You don’t need to attempt Acrobatics checks to move at full speed while blinded.

data.Name: Bodyguard
data.Short Description: Add a +2 bonus to an adjacent ally’s AC as a reaction
data.Benefit: As a reaction when an adjacent ally is attacked, you can grant that ally a +2 circumstance bonus to its AC against that attack. If you do so, you take a –2 penalty to your own Armor Class until the beginning of your next turn.

data.Name: In Harm’s Way
data.Short Description: Take the damage of a successful attack against an adjacent ally
data.Benefit: When an attack hits an ally whose Armor Class you have increased with the Bodyguard feat, you can intercept the attack. You take all damage and associated effects from that attack, and once you intercept the attack, no other ability can redirect it. This ability takes no action, but you can intercept only one attack in this way each round.

data.Name: Cleave
data.Short Description: Make an additional melee attack if the first one hits
data.Benefit: As a standard action, you can make a single melee attack against a foe within reach. If you hit, you deal damage normally and can make an additional melee attack (using your full base attack bonus) against a foe that is adjacent to the first and also within reach. You can make only one additional attack per round with this feat. When you use this feat, you take a –2 penalty to your Armor Class until your next turn.

data.Name: Great Cleave
data.Prerequisites: Str 13, Feat Cleave, base attack bonus +4
data.Short Description: Make an additional melee attack after each melee attack that hits
data.Benefit: If you strike a second target with the Cleave feat, you can spend 1 Resolve Point to make a melee attack against each subsequent foe that is both adjacent to the last target and within your reach, as long as you hit the previous foe. You can’t attack an individual foe more than once during this attack action.

data.Name: Climbing Master
data.Short Description: Gain a climb speed equal to your land speed
data.Benefit: You gain a climb speed equal to your land speed.

data.Name: Combat Casting
data.Short Description: +2 bonus to AC and saves against attacks of opportunity when casting spells
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 bonus to your Armor Class and saving throws against attacks of opportunity you provoked by casting a spell and against readied actions triggered by your spellcasting.

data.Name: Connection Inkling
data.Prerequisites: Wis 15, character level 5th, no mystic levels
data.Short Description: Gain the ability to cast minor mystic spells
data.Benefit: Choose two 0-level mystic spells and one 1st-level mystic spell. You can cast the 0-level spells at will and the 1stlevel spell once per day for every 3 character levels you have. For these spells, your caster level is equal to your character level and the key ability score is Wisdom. If you later gain levels in mystic, you lose the benefits of this feat and can replace it with either Spell Focus or Spell Penetration.

data.Name: Coordinated Shot
data.Short Description: Allies gain a +1 bonus to ranged attacks against foes you threaten
data.Benefit: When you are threatening a foe with a melee weapon, any ally that has line of sight to that foe without you granting that foe cover gains a +1 bonus to ranged attack rolls against that foe.

data.Name: Deadly Aim
data.Short Description: Take a -2 penalty to weapon attacks to deal extra damage
data.Benefit: When you take the attack or full attack action with weapons (including a solarian’s solar manifestation, but not spells or other special abilities of any kind), you can take a –2 penalty to your attack rolls. If you do, those attacks deal additional damage equal to half your base attack bonus (minimum 1).

data.Name: Deflect Projectiles
data.Short Description: Spend 1 Resolve Point to attempt to avoid a ranged attack
data.Benefit: When you would be hit by a ranged attack from a weapon or spell that deals energy or kinetic damage, you can spend 1 Resolve Point as a reaction to make an attack roll with a nonarchaic melee weapon that deals the same general category of damage (energy or kinetic) with a +5 bonus. If your attack roll is higher than the attack roll that hit you, you deflect the attack with your weapon, and it misses. This doesn’t work against area attacks, even against area attacks that have attack rolls like blast weapons, and you can’t use this reaction if you aren’t capable of making an attack with an appropriate weapon.

data.Name: Reflect Projectiles
data.Short Description: Spend 1 Resolve Point to attempt to redirect a ranged attack
data.Benefit: When you successfully deflect an attack with the Deflect Projectiles feat, you can spend 1 additional Resolve Point to redirect the attack at a target within 60 feet to which you have line of effect. Make a ranged attack at a –4 penalty against the appropriate Armor Class of the new target. If your attack succeeds, the target is damaged as if it had been the target of the original attack.

data.Name: Diehard
data.Short Description: You can spend Resolve Points to stabilize and to stay in the fight in the same round
data.Normal: You must use Resolve Points to stabilize and to stay in the fight in separate rounds.
data.Benefit: When you are dying, you can spend the required Resolve Points to stabilize and 1 Resolve Point to stay in the fight (regaining 1 Hit Point) in the same round.

data.Name: Dive for Cover
data.Short Description: Fall prone in an adjacent square to roll a Reflex save twice
data.Benefit: When you attempt a Reflex save against an area attack or area effect, you can fall prone in an adjacent square and roll that Reflex save twice (taking the better result of the two). If you were in a threatened space, this movement provokes attacks of opportunity normally.

data.Name: Diversion
data.Short Description: Use Bluff to create a distraction so that your allies can hide
data.Normal: Using Bluff to create a distraction allows only you to attempt a Stealth check to hide.
data.Benefit: When you successfully use Bluff to create a distraction, you can allow an ally to attempt a Stealth check to hide (instead of attempting to hide yourself). You can attempt to allow multiple allies to use Stealth to hide, but you take a –5 penalty to your Bluff check for every ally after the first, and on a failed check no ally can attempt to hide.

data.Name: Drag Down
data.Short Description: When you are tripped, you can attempt to trip an adjacent foe
data.Benefit: Whenever an opponent successfully uses the trip combat maneuver against you, you can attempt to trip an adjacent opponent as a reaction.

data.Name: Enhanced Resistance
data.Short Description: Gain damage reduction or energy resistance
data.Benefit: Choose one kinetic damage type (bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing) or one energy type (acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic). If you choose a kinetic damage, you ignore damage of that type equal to your base attack bonus; this doesn’t stack with DR. If you choose an energy type, you gain energy resistance against that type of energy equal to your base attack bonus.

data.Name: Extra Resolve
data.Short Description: Gain 2 additional Resolve Points
data.Benefit: You have 2 additional Resolve Points in your pool.

data.Name: Far Shot
data.Short Description: Reduce penalty due to range increments
data.Normal: You take a –2 penalty per full range increment between you and your target.
data.Benefit: You take only a –1 penalty per full range increment between you and your target when using a ranged weapon.

data.Name: Fast Talk
data.Short Description: Baffle a potential foe, causing it to be surprised when combat begins
data.Benefit: You can distract a creature with a confusing barrage of words, causing it to be surprised at the beginning of combat. You can use this ability only on a creature you are able to converse with (it must be able to see or hear you and understand your words) prior to the beginning of combat, and you must continue to converse with it until combat begins. You can’t use this ability if you are the one to instigate combat or if you are unaware at the start of combat. When the GM declares that combat has begun, but before initiative is rolled, you can spend 1 Resolve Point to attempt a Bluff check against a single creature that this ability can affect. The DC is equal to 15 + the target’s total Perception skill bonus, or 20 + one and a half × the target’s CR, whichever is higher. If your check is successful, the target creature is considered unaware at the start of combat, allowing other creatures (including yourself) to act in a surprise round. Once you have attempted to use this ability on a creature, whether or not you succeed, it is immune to this ability for 24 hours.

data.Name: Fleet
data.Short Description: Increase your land speed
data.Benefit: While you are wearing light or no armor, your land speed increases by 10 feet. When you are encumbered, your land speed instead increases by only 5 feet. When you are overburdened, your speed is reduced to 10 feet.

data.Name: Fusillade
data.Prerequisites: Base attack bonus +1, 4 or more arms
data.Short Description: Make an automatic-mode attack with multiple small arms
data.Benefit: As a full attack when you are wielding four or more identical small arms, you can fire them all simultaneously to duplicate the effects of an automatic weapon (see page 180). You use all of the ammunition in all the small arms used, and you treat this as an attack in automatic mode. Add all the ammunition expended from all of your small arms when determining the maximum number of creatures you can hit.

data.Name: Great Fortitude
data.Short Description: +2 bonus to Fortitude saves
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 bonus to Fortitude saving throws.

data.Name: Improved Great Fortitude
data.Short Description: Spend 1 Resolve Point to reroll a Fortitude save
data.Benefit: You can spend 1 RP to reroll a failed Fortitude save (see page 243).

data.Name: Grenade Proficiency
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with grenades
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency with grenades (see Weapon Proficiency on page 243).

data.Name: Harm Undead
data.Prerequisites: Healing channel connection power, mystic level 1st
data.Short Description: Expend a spell slot for healing channel to also damage undead
data.Benefit: When you use your healing channel as a full action, you can expend a mystic spell slot of the highest level you can cast to also deal damage equal to the amount you heal to all undead foes in the area. The undead can attempt a Will save for half damage, at your usual connection power DC.

data.Name: Heavy Armor Proficiency
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with heavy armor
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency with heavy armor (see page 242).

data.Name: Heavy Weapon Proficiency
data.Prerequisites: Str 13, proficiency with longarms and small arms
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with heavy weapons
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency with heavy weapons (see page 243).

data.Name: Improved Combat Maneuver
data.Short Description: +4 bonus to perform one combat maneuver
data.Special: You can take Improved Combat Maneuver multiple times. The effects don’t stack. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a new combat maneuver.
data.Benefit: Choose one combat maneuver (bull rush, dirty trick, disarm, grapple, reposition, sunder, or trip). You gain a +4 bonus to your attack roll to resolve that combat maneuver.

data.Name: Pull the Pin
data.Short Description: Perform a disarm to activate a foe’s grenade
data.Benefit: When you make a successful disarm combat maneuver against a foe that you know has grenades, rather than disarming the foe of a weapon, you can activate one grenade in the foe’s possession. You can activate only a grenade that is ready to be drawn and thrown (not, for example, a grenade stowed away within an equipment pack). The grenade explodes at the end of your current turn, unless it has a delayed fuse that causes it to go off 1 round or more after it is activated. The foe takes a –2 penalty to the saving throw against this grenade, and the grenade’s explosion has half its normal area.

data.Name: Improved Critical
data.Short Description: The DC to resist the critical effects of your critical hits increases by 2
data.Benefit: When you score a critical hit with a weapon, increase the DC to resist that weapon’s critical effect by 2.

data.Name: Improved Feint
data.Short Description: Use Bluff to feint as a move action
data.Benefit: You can use Bluff to feint in combat as a move action.

data.Name: Greater Feint
data.Prerequisites: Feat Improved Feint, base attack bonus +6
data.Short Description: Foes you feint against are flat-footed for 1 round
data.Benefit: Whenever you successfully feint in combat, the foe gains the flat-footed condition until the end of your next turn.

data.Name: Improved Initiative
data.Short Description: +4 bonus to initiative checks
data.Benefit: You gain a +4 bonus to initiative checks.

data.Name: Improved Unarmed Strike
data.Short Description: Deal more damage and threaten squares with unarmed strikes
data.Normal: You don’t threaten any squares with unarmed strikes, and you must have a hand free to make an unarmed strike.
data.Benefit: Your unarmed strike damage increases to 1d6 at 4th level, 2d6 at 8th level, 3d6 at 12th level, 5d6 at 15th level, and 7d6 at 20th level. Your unarmed strikes don’t count as archaic and can deal lethal damage. You threaten squares within your natural reach with your unarmed strikes even when you do not have a hand free for an unarmed strike. If you are immobilized, entangled, or unable to use both legs (or whatever appendages you have in place of legs, where appropriate), you lose the ability to make unarmed strikes without your hands. When making an unarmed strike without your hands, you can’t use such attacks for combat maneuvers or similar abilities—only to deal damage.

data.Name: Iron Will
data.Short Description: +2 bonus to Will saves
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 bonus to Will saving throws.

data.Name: Improved Iron Will
data.Short Description: Spend 1 Resolve Point to reroll a Will save
data.Benefit: You can spend 1 RP to reroll a failed Will save (see page 243).

data.Name: Jet Dash
data.Short Description: Move faster when running, double height and distance when jumping
data.Normal: You move four times your land speed and gain the flat-footed condition while running.
data.Benefit: When running, you move six times your land speed. Whenever you jump, double the height and distance you can jump. While running, you don’t gain the flat-footed condition.

data.Name: Kip Up
data.Short Description: Stand from prone as a swift action
data.Benefit: You can stand from prone as a swift action, rather than a move action.

data.Name: Light Armor Proficiency
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with light armor
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency with light armor (see Armor Proficiency on page 242).

data.Name: Lightning Reflexes
data.Short Description: +2 bonus to Reflex saves
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 bonus to Reflex saving throws.

data.Name: Improved Lightning Reflexes
data.Short Description: Spend 1 Resolve Point to reroll a Reflex save
data.Benefit: You can spend 1 RP to reroll a failed Reflex save (see page 243).

data.Name: Longarm Proficiency
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with longarms
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency with longarms (see Weapon Proficiency on page 243).

data.Name: Lunge
data.Short Description: Increase reach of melee attacks by 5 feet until the end of your turn
data.Benefit: You can increase the reach of your melee attacks by 5 feet until the end of your turn by taking a –2 penalty to your Armor Class until the beginning of your next turn. You must decide to use this ability before making any attacks in the round.

data.Name: Master Crafter
data.Prerequisites: Computers, Engineering, Life Science, Mysticism, Physical Science, or Profession 5 ranks
data.Short Description: Craft items in half the normal time
data.Special: You can take Master Crafter multiple times. Each time you do, it applies to a different skill that meets the prerequisite number of ranks.
data.Benefit: Choose one skill that allows you to craft items in which you have the required number of ranks. When you craft items using that skill, it takes half the normal time.

data.Name: Medical Expert
data.Prerequisites: Life Science 1 rank, Medicine 1 rank, Physical Science 1 rank
data.Short Description: Treat deadly wounds more quickly, and provide long-term care without a medical lab
data.Normal: It takes 1 minute to treat deadly wounds, and longterm care requires a medical bay or medical lab.
data.Benefit: You can use the Medicine skill in conjunction with a medpatch or sprayflesh to treat deadly wounds as a full action (DC 25). If you have an ability that enables you to treat deadly wounds more often but with a longer time frame (such as the envoy’s surgeon expertise talent), you cannot use this feat to use that ability more quickly. When used in this way, the medpatch or sprayflesh does not perform any of its normal functions. You can also use the Medicine skill to provide long-term care with just a medkit.

data.Name: Minor Psychic Power
data.Short Description: Cast a 0-level spell as a spell-like ability 3/day
data.Special: You can select this feat more than once. Each time you do, you must choose a different spell from the list.
data.Benefit: Select one of the following 0-level spells; dancing lights, psychokinetic hand, telekinetic projectile, or telepathic message. You can cast this spell three times per day as a spell-like ability, using your character level as your caster level.

data.Name: Psychic Power
data.Prerequisites: Cha 13, Feat Minor Psychic Power, character level 4th
data.Short Description: Cast a 1st-level spell as a spell-like ability 1/day
data.Special: You can select this feat more than once. Each time you do, you must choose a different spell from the list.
data.Benefit: Select one of the following 1st-level spells; comprehend languages, detect thoughts, or mindlink. You can cast this spell once per day as a spell-like ability, using your character level as your caster level.

data.Name: Major Psychic Power
data.Prerequisites: Cha 15, Feat Minor Psychic Power, Feat Psychic Power, character level 7th
data.Short Description: Cast a 2nd-level spell as a spell-like ability 1/day
data.Benefit: Select one of the following 2nd-level spells: augury or status. You can cast this spell once per day as a spell-like ability, using your character level as your caster level.

data.Name: Mobility
data.Short Description: +4 bonus to AC against attacks of opportunity from movement
data.Benefit: You gain a +4 bonus to your Armor Class against attacks of opportunity that you provoke by leaving a threatened square.

data.Name: Agile Casting
data.Prerequisites: Key ability score 15, Dex 15, Feat Mobility, caster level 4th
data.Short Description: Cast a spell at any point during movement
data.Normal: You can move only before or after casting a spell, not both.
data.Benefit: As a full action, you can move up to your speed and cast a single spell with a casting time of one standard action or less at any point during your movement. If you have a supernatural ability that can be activated as a standard action or less, you can instead use that ability at any point during your movement.

data.Name: Shot on the Run
data.Short Description: base attack bonus +4 Make a ranged attack at any point during movement
data.Normal: You can move only before or after making a ranged attack, not both.
data.Benefit: As a full action, you can move up to your speed and make a single ranged attack at any point during your movement. If you have the trick attack class feature, you can take your movement from trick attack at any time during a trick attack (see page 93) with a ranged weapon (instead of only before).

data.Name: Parting Shot
data.Prerequisites: Dex 15, Mobility, Feat Shot on the Run, base attack bonus +6
data.Short Description: Make a single ranged attack when withdrawing
data.Normal: You can’t attack when using the withdraw action.
data.Benefit: When using the withdraw action, you can make a single ranged attack at any point during your movement. If you have the trick attack class feature and are using an appropriate weapon, you can add your trick attack damage to the attack you make while withdrawing. Once you use this ability, you can’t use it again until the next time you regain Stamina Points with a 10-minute rest.

data.Name: Sidestep
data.Prerequisites: Dex 15, Feat Mobility or trick attack class feature
data.Short Description: Take guarded step as a reaction when a foe misses you with melee attack
data.Benefit: Whenever an opponent misses you with a melee attack, you can take a guarded step as a reaction, as long as you remain within that opponent’s threatened area. If you take this step, you can’t take a guarded step during your next turn. If you take an action to move during your next turn, subtract 5 feet from your total movement.

data.Name: Improved Sidestep
data.Prerequisites: Dex 17, Feat Mobility or trick attack class feature, Feat Sidestep
data.Short Description: Reduce penalties from Sidestep
data.Normal: If you use the Sidestep feat, you can’t take a guarded step during your next turn and your movement is reduced by 5 feet during your next turn.
data.Benefit: After sidestepping an opponent’s missed attack using the Sidestep feat, you can still take a guarded step during your next turn, or you can move up to your full speed if you take an action to move during your next turn.

data.Name: Spring Attack
data.Prerequisites: Dex 15, Feat Mobility, base attack bonus +4
data.Short Description: Move before and after a melee attack
data.Normal: You can move only before or after an attack, not both.
data.Benefit: As a full action, you can move up to your speed and make a single melee attack or combat maneuver without provoking any attacks of opportunity from the target of your attack. You can move both before and after the attack, but you must move at least 10 feet before the attack, and the total distance that you move cannot be greater than your speed. You cannot use this ability to attack a foe that is adjacent to you at the start of your turn. If you have the trick attack class feature, you can take your movement from trick attack at any time during a trick attack with a melee weapon (instead of only before), without provoking any attacks of opportunity from the target of your attack.

data.Name: Multi-Weapon Fighting
data.Short Description: Reduce the penalty for full attacks when using multiple small arms or operative melee weapons
data.Benefit: When you make a full attack with two or more small arms or with two or more operative melee weapons (see page 184), reduce the penalty for making a full attack by 1.

data.Name: Mystic Strike
data.Short Description: Melee and ranged attacks count as magic
data.Benefit: Your melee and ranged attacks count as magic for the purposes of bypassing damage reduction and other situations, such as attacking incorporeal creatures.

data.Name: Nimble Moves
data.Short Description: Ignore 20 feet of difficult terrain when you move
data.Benefit: You can move through up to 20 feet of difficult terrain each round as if it were normal terrain. This feat allows you to take a guarded step into difficult terrain.

data.Name: Opening Volley
data.Short Description: +2 bonus to a melee attack against a target you damaged with a ranged attack
data.Benefit: Whenever you deal damage to an opponent with a ranged attack on your first turn in a combat, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your next melee attack roll against that opponent. This melee attack must occur before the end of your next turn.

data.Name: Penetrating Attack
data.Short Description: Reduce enemy's DR and energy resistance against your weapons by 5
data.Benefit: Reduce your target’s energy resistances and damage reduction by 5 against your weapon attacks.

data.Name: Penetrating Spell
data.Short Description: Reduce enemy's DR and energy resistance against your spells by 5
data.Benefit: Reduce your target’s energy resistances and damage reduction by 5 against your spell damage.

data.Name: Powered Armor Proficiency
data.Prerequisites: Str 13, proficiency with light armor and heavy armor, base attack bonus +5
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with powered armor
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency with powered armor (see Armor Proficiency on page 242).

data.Name: Quick Draw
data.Short Description: Draw a weapon as a swift action
data.Normal: You can draw a weapon as a move action, or (if your base attack bonus is +1 or higher) as part of a move action, and you can draw a hidden weapon as a standard action.
data.Benefit: You can draw a weapon as a swift action. Additionally, when making an attack using a thrown weapon as an attack or full attack action, you can draw a weapon as part of the action of making a thrown attack with it. You can draw a hidden weapon (see Sleight of Hand on page 146) as a move action.

data.Name: Skill Focus
data.Short Description: +3 insight bonus to one skill
data.Special: You can take this feat multiple times. Its effects don’t stack. Each time you take this feat, it applies to a new skill.
data.Benefit: Choose a skill. You gain a +3 insight bonus to checks involving the chosen skill.

data.Name: Skill Synergy
data.Short Description: Gain two new class skills or a +2 insight bonus to those skills
data.Special: You can take this feat multiple times. Its effects don’t stack. Each time you take it, it applies to two different skills.
data.Benefit: Choose two skills. These skills become class skills for you. If one or both were already class skills, you gain a +2 insight bonus to those skill checks instead.

data.Name: Sky Jockey
data.Short Description: Make jetpacks, vehicles, and starships go faster
data.Benefit: When you’re using a device to fly (including magic items, but not spells or natural flight), your fly speed increases by 10 feet. If you are operating a flying vehicle, its fly speed increases by 10 (though this has no impact on its full speed or overland movement speed). When you are in the pilot role of starship combat, your starship’s speed increases by 1.

data.Name: Slippery Shooter
data.Short Description: +3 bonus to AC against attacks of opportunity when making ranged attacks
data.Benefit: You gain a +3 bonus to your Armor Class against attacks of opportunity you provoke by making a ranged attack.

data.Name: Small Arm Proficiency
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with small arms
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency in small arms (see Weapon Proficiency on page 243).

data.Name: Sniper Weapon Proficiency
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with sniper weapons
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency in sniper weapons (see Weapon Proficiency on page 243).

data.Name: Special Weapon Proficiency
data.Prerequisites: Proficiency with basic melee weapons or small arms
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with one special weapon
data.Special: You can take this feat multiple times. Each time you do, select a different special weapon.
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency with a single special weapon of your choice (see Weapon Proficiency on page 243). If the special weapon you choose is a melee special weapon, you must have proficiency with basic melee weapons. If the special weapon you choose is a ranged weapon, you must have proficiency with small arms. If a set of special weapons are all different models of the same weapon, proficiency with one such weapon grants proficiency with all weapons in the set.

data.Name: Spell Focus
data.Prerequisites: Ability to cast spells, character level 3rd
data.Short Description: DCs of spells you cast increase
data.Benefit: The DC of spells you cast increases by 1. At 11th level, the DC of your spells instead increases by 2, and at 17th level, the DC of spells you cast instead increases by 3. This bonus does not apply to spell-like abilities.

data.Name: Spell Penetration
data.Short Description: +2 bonus to caster level checks to overcome SR
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 bonus to caster level checks to overcome a creature’s spell resistance (see page 265).

data.Name: Greater Spell Penetration
data.Short Description: Penetration Additional +2 bonus to caster level checks to overcome SR
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 bonus to caster level checks to overcome a creature’s spell resistance. This bonus stacks with the bonus from Spell Penetration.

data.Name: Spellbane
data.Prerequisites: Unable to cast spells or use spell-like abilities
data.Short Description: +2 insight bonus to saving throws against spells and spell-like abilities
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 insight bonus to saving throws against spells and spell-like abilities. If you ever gain the ability to cast spells or use spell-like abilities, you lose the benefits of this feat and can replace it with Great Fortitude, Lightning Reflexes, or Iron Will, or the improved version of one of those feats if you meet its prerequisites.

data.Name: Spry Cover
data.Short Description: Covering fire grants a +4 bonus to an ally’s Acrobatics check to tumble
data.Benefit: Instead of the usual benefits of covering fire, you can use covering fire to grant an ally a +4 bonus to her next Acrobatics check to tumble before the end of your next turn.

data.Name: Stand Still
data.Short Description: Make an attack of opportunity to stop a foe’s movement
data.Benefit: When a foe provokes an attack of opportunity by leaving a square you threaten, you can attempt a melee attack as a reaction against that foe’s Kinetic Armor Class + 8. If you’re successful, the enemy cannot take any further movement for the rest of its turn. The enemy can still take the rest of its actions, but cannot leave that square.

data.Name: Improved Stand Still
data.Short Description: +4 bonus to melee attacks with Stand Still
data.Benefit: You gain a +4 bonus to your melee attack roll for Stand Still.

data.Name: Step Up
data.Short Description: Take a guarded step as a reaction to an adjacent foe moving
data.Benefit: Whenever an adjacent foe attempts to take a guarded step away from you, you can also take a guarded step as a reaction as long as you end up adjacent to the foe that triggered this ability.

data.Name: Step Up and Strike
data.Prerequisites: Dex 13, Feat Step Up, base attack bonus +6
data.Short Description: Make an attack of opportunity as part of Step Up
data.Benefit: When using the Step Up feat to follow an adjacent foe, you can move up to 10 feet. You can also either make an attack of opportunity against the foe, or wait to see if the foe provokes another attack of opportunity at any point before the end of its turn. Either way, this attack of opportunity does not count toward the number of actions you can usually take each round; it is part of the Step Up reaction.

data.Name: Strike Back
data.Short Description: Ready an action to make a melee attack against a foe with reach
data.Benefit: You can ready an action to make a melee attack against a foe that attacks you with a melee weapon, even if that foe isn’t within your reach.

data.Name: Suppressive Fire
data.Prerequisites: Base attack bonus +1, proficiency with heavy weapons
data.Short Description: Provide covering fire or harrying fire in an area
data.Benefit: As a full action, you can use a ranged weapon with the automatic weapon property to provide covering fire or harrying fire in a cone with a range equal to half the weapon’s range increment. You must expend 10 charges or rounds of ammunition to use this ability. Decide if you are providing covering fire or harrying fire. Make a single ranged attack roll with a +4 bonus, and compare it to the AC of all creatures in the area. Any creature with an AC equal to or less than your attack roll is affected by the selected effect. If you select covering fire, choose a single ally that gains the benefit of the covering fire bonus to its AC.

data.Name: Swimming Master
data.Short Description: Gain a swim speed equal to your land speed
data.Benefit: You gain a swim speed equal to your land speed.

data.Name: Technomantic Dabbler
data.Prerequisites: Int 15, character level 5th, no levels in technomancer
data.Short Description: Gain the ability to cast minor technomancer spells
data.Benefit: Choose two 0-level technomancer spells and one 1st-level technomancer spell. You can cast the 0-level spells at will and the 1st-level spell once per day for every 3 character levels you have. For these spells, your caster level is equal to your character level and the key ability score is Intelligence. If you later gain levels in technomancer, you lose the benefits of this feat and can replace it with either Spell Focus or Spell Penetration.

data.Name: Toughness
data.Short Description: +1 Stamina Point per character level and other bonuses
data.Benefit: For every character level you have (and whenever you gain a new level), you gain 1 Stamina Point. In addition, you gain a +4 bonus to Constitution checks to continue running, to avoid damage from a forced march, to hold your breath, and to avoid damage from starvation or thirst. You also gain a +4 bonus to Fortitude saving throws to avoid taking damage from hot or cold environments, to withstand the harmful effects of thick and thin atmospheres, to avoid choking when breathing in heavy smoke, and to avoid fatigue caused by sleep deprivation.

data.Name: Unfriendly Fire
data.Short Description: Trick an attacker into shooting at another enemy adjacent to you
data.Benefit: As a reaction when an attacker makes a ranged attack against you and misses, you can attempt a Bluff check to redirect the attack toward a different target. The new target must be adjacent to you and within line of sight and line of effect of the original attacker. The DC for this check is equal to either 10 + the attacker’s total Sense Motive skill bonus, or 15 + one and a half × the attacker’s CR, whichever is greater. You can’t take 10 on this check. If you succeed, the original attacker rerolls the attack roll (see page 243) with the same bonuses against the appropriate Armor Class of the new target. Once a creature has observed you use this feat, even if that creature is neither the attacker nor the new target, you can’t use this feat to attempt to redirect attacks from that creature for 24 hours.

data.Name: Veiled Threat
data.Short Description: Intimidated foe doesn’t become hostile
data.Benefit: When you successfully use the Intimidate skill to bully a creature, after the duration of its helpful attitude ends, its attitude toward you becomes indifferent rather than hostile.

data.Name: Weapon Focus
data.Short Description: +1 bonus to attack rolls with selected weapon type
data.Benefit: Chose one weapon type (small arms, longarms, heavy weapons, etc.). You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls with that weapon type. If your base attack bonus is at least 3 lower than your character level (or your mechanic’s class level, if you are a drone), you gain a +2 bonus instead.

data.Name: Versatile Focus
data.Short Description: +1 bonus to attack rolls with all weapon types you are proficient with
data.Benefit: The benefits of Weapon Focus extend to all weapons with which you are proficient.

data.Name: Weapon Specialization
data.Prerequisites: Character level 3rd, proficiency with selected weapon type
data.Short Description: Deal extra damage with selected weapon type
data.Benefit: Choose one weapon type (small arms, longarms, heavy weapons, etc.). You gain specialization in that weapon type, which means you add your character level to damage with the selected weapon type, or half your character level for small arms or operative melee weapons. You can never have specialization in grenades.

data.Name: Versatile Specialization
data.Short Description: Deal extra damage with all weapon types you are proficient with
data.Benefit: You gain specialization (see page 243) in all weapons with which you are proficient that can be selected with Weapon Specialization.

data.Name: Accelerated Recovery
data.Short Description: Recover more quickly during an 8-hour rest
data.Benefit: You regain twice as many Hit Points when taking an 8-hour rest. In addition, for each uninterrupted 8 hours of rest you receive, you recover from afflictions as though you had rested for a full day. This effect does not stack with other effects that increase these rates of healing and recovery; if more than one would apply, you choose which one takes effect.

data.Name: Adaptive Casting
data.Short Description: Select three additional spells known; cast one of them once a day
data.Benefit: Select three spells from class spell lists you can cast spells from. They must be at least 1 level lower than the highest spell level you can cast. Once per day, you can cast one of these spells as a spell-like ability. Each time you gain a caster level, you can change which three spells you have selected with this feat.

data.Name: Adaptive Upgrade
data.Short Description: Temporarily create low-level armor upgrades
data.Benefit: Select three armor upgrades, each with an item level no greater than half your ranks in Engineering. If these upgrades require any choices to be made (such as what kind of energy damage they protect against), you must make those choices when you select this feat. With 8 hours of work, you can adapt any armor upgrade of an item level equal to at least half your ranks of Engineering, allowing it to function as one of your three selected upgrades. This upgrade functions only when the upgrade is installed in armor you are wearing. Activating this upgrade requires a move action, after which the upgrade functions as both its normal upgrade and your selected upgrade for 1 minute. If a selected upgrade requires charges, ammunition, or other resources, you must provide them normally; you can add batteries and ammunition to the upgrade as part of the process of adapting it to work with your selected upgrade choices. You can have only one upgrade adapted to be usable with this feat at a time—if you adapt a new upgrade, the adaptations to any previous upgrade are lost. Once you’ve used your adapted upgrade, you can’t use it again until you’ve regained Stamina Points after a 10-minute rest. Each time you gain another rank in Engineering, you can change the three upgrades you have selected with this feat.

data.Name: Add Leverage
data.Short Description: Grip your weapon with more hands to move foes farther with combat maneuvers
data.Benefit: For each hand you use to wield your weapon beyond the minimum required to wield that weapon (to a maximum of two extra hands), you increase the distance you can move your opponent with a successful bull rush or reposition combat maneuver by 5 feet. If you use a trip combat maneuver against a flying opponent, you can increase the distance that creature descends by 5 feet for each additional hand used to wield your weapon. Changing your grip to alter the number of hands you are wielding a weapon with is a swift action. You can’t hold items in hands used to add leverage, or use them for any other purpose.

data.Name: Advanced Warning
data.Short Description: Shout a warning to your allies, causing them to stop being flat-footed
data.Benefit: As a move action, you can shout a warning to your allies, ending the flat-footed condition for any ally within 60 feet. You cannot use this feat when you’re flat-footed. Once you’ve used this ability, you cannot do so again until you’ve regained Stamina Points after a 10-minute rest. This is a sense-dependent ability.

data.Name: Ambuscade
data.Short Description: When you make an attack in the surprise round against a target that has not yet acted, gain a bonus to attacks and damage
data.Benefit: When you act in a surprise round and attack a target that has not yet acted in the same combat, you gain a +1 bonus to your attack roll and a bonus to damage equal to half your base attack bonus.

data.Name: Ambush Awareness
data.Short Description: If you fail the Perception check to act in the surprise round, you can still take a full defense action
data.Benefit: If you are unable to act in the surprise round because you failed a Perception check, you can still act on your initiative count in the surprise round, but only to take the total defense action.

data.Name: Blood in Their Eyes
data.Short Description: Your critical hits with slashing attacks make foes dazzled
data.Benefit: When you score a critical hit with a weapon that deals slashing damage, in addition to any other critical hit effect you would normally apply, you also impose the dazzled condition on the target. The condition lasts until ended by any action or effect that would end the bleeding condition.

data.Name: Constant Alert
data.Short Description: Reroll your initiative check and automatically win ties
data.Benefit: Once per day, you can reroll an initiative check. You also win tied initiative checks as though you had the higher initiative bonus. If multiple characters have this feat, ties are resolved normally.

data.Name: Cook Grenade
data.Short Description: Time your release of a grenade to increase its effectiveness
data.Benefit: As a full action, you can draw a grenade and make a ranged attack with it. If you do so, the grenade’s Reflex save DC is increased by 2. If your attack roll is a natural 1 (the d20 comes up 1), the grenade instead detonates at a randomly determined grid intersection of your space.

data.Name: Cosmic Truth
data.Short Description: Reveal a cosmic truth that makes one target who fails a Will save confused for 1d4 rounds
data.Benefit: As a standard action, you can spend 1 RP to force one creature within 30 feet of you to attempt a Will save (DC = 10 + half your ranks in Bluff + your Wisdom bonus); if it fails, it is confused for 1d4 rounds. Once a creature has seen or heard you use this ability, it is immune to your further uses of the ability for 24 hours. This is a language-dependent, sense-dependent ability.

data.Name: Deadly Boast
data.Short Description: Make an attack or effect sound so impressive that foes have a harder time defending against it
data.Benefit: As a full action, you can describe in great detail how powerful and effective a specific action taken by you or an ally is going to be. You must select a specific character to boast about, and a specific weapon, item, spell, or ability to be used. Attempt a Bluff check against all foes within 60 feet of you, using the same DC you would use if attempting to demoralize them with Intimidate—roll a single check and compare the result to the DC for each target. Each target your check succeeds against takes a –1 penalty to AC and a –2 penalty to saving throws against the action you boasted about until the end of your next turn. Once you have attempted to use this ability against a creature, it is immune to your use of this feat for 24 hours. This is a sensedependent, language-dependent ability.

data.Name: Defensive Roll
data.Short Description: Attempt an Acrobatics check in place of a Reflex save or to dodge an attack
data.Benefit: As a reaction when you are attacked or when you must attempt a Reflex saving throw, you can attempt an Acrobatics check. If you use this ability as a reaction to an attack and your Acrobatics check result exceeds your opponent’s attack roll result, the attack misses you. If you use this ability as a reaction when you must attempt a Reflex saving throw, you can use your Acrobatics check result as your Reflex save result. Once you use this ability, you cannot do so again until you regain Stamina Points after a 10-minute rest. After you use this ability, you are staggered on your next turn.

data.Name: Dire Straits
data.Short Description: Stabilize when you have no Hit Points or Resolve Points left
data.Benefit: Once per day, when you are reduced to 0 Hit Points and have no Resolve Points remaining, you immediately stabilize.

data.Name: Disease Adaptation
data.Short Description: +2 enhancement bonus to saves against disease
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 enhancement bonus to saving throws against disease.

data.Name: Disease Rejection
data.Short Description: Recover from diseases much more swiftly
data.Benefit: When you fulfill a disease’s cure condition, you move two steps toward healthy instead of one.

data.Name: Dispelling Strike
data.Short Description: Spend 1 Resolve Point to make a melee attack act as dispel magic
data.Benefit: When you are wielding a magic weapon (normally a weapon with a weapon fusion, but anything that bypasses DR-magic qualifies), as a standard action you can spend 1 Resolve Point to cast dispel magic for the targeted dispel function. You can use this only against an adjacent target. Once you have used this ability, you cannot do so again until you regain Stamina Points after a 10-minute rest.

data.Name: Double Tap
data.Prerequisites: Feat Weapon Focus (small arms), proficiency with small arms
data.Short Description: Expend double the normal ammunition to gain bonuses to attack and damage with a small arm
data.Benefit: As a standard action, you can take a single attack action to make a double-tap attack with a small arm. This attack gains a +1 bonus to the attack roll, and your bonus to damage from Weapon Specialization is equal to your character level (rather than half your character level). The small arm used cannot have the blast, explode, flexible lineAR, line, unwieldy, or wide lineAR weapon special properties, or any other property that allows it to attack multiple targets or an area in a single attack. This attack cannot benefit from the boost, guidedAR, or variant boostAR weapon special properties, or any other ability or effect that is a move action and alters the effect of your attack or damage. This expends ammunition equal to two attack rolls, and if you cannot expend that ammunition, you cannot use this ability.

data.Name: Echolocation Attack
data.Short Description: Use the echo of sonic attacks to better sense your surroundings
data.Benefit: When you take an action that deals sonic damage, as a move action you can grant yourself blindsense (sound) with a range of 30 feet until the end of your next turn. If you have blindsense (sound or vibration), you can instead grant yourself blindsight (sound or vibration, to match your blindsense) with a range of 30 feet or a range equal to your blindsense (whichever is shorter) until the end of your next turn.

data.Name: Environmental Adaptation
data.Short Description: Gain immunity to a number of environmental and weather effects
data.Benefit: Select a number of the following environmental conditions and weather conditions (Core Rulebook 395) equal to your Constitution modifier; thick atmosphere (includes severely thick atmosphere), thin atmosphere (includes severely thin atmosphere), toxic atmosphere (includes low-level and heavily toxic atmospheres), rain and snow (includes rain, snow, heavy snow, and dust storms), winds (includes light, moderate, strong, and severe winds and windstorms), cold dangers (includes cold, severe cold, and extreme cold), extreme gravity, heat dangers (includes heat, severe heat, and extreme heat), and smoke effects (including smoke grenades). You do not take any of the selected conditions’ penalties to skill checks or attack rolls, do not take nonlethal damage from them, and do not need to attempt saving throws to resist their effects. Once these condition choices are made, they cannot be changed. If your Constitution bonus increases from an effect that does not have a limited duration, you can add additional conditions to your list.

data.Name: Focused Sense
data.Short Description: Temporarily gain blindsight with a limited range
data.Benefit: As a move action, you can gain blindsight (using the same sense as your blindsense) with a range equal to half that of your blindsense until the beginning of your next turn. You can use this ability with only one type of blindsense (such as sound or vibration) at a time.

data.Name: Fouling Reposition
data.Short Description: Reposition a foe to throw it and another creature off balance
data.Benefit: When you succeed at a reposition combat maneuver against a creature and move your target so it ends its movement adjacent to a second creature, both creatures become off-target until the beginning of your next turn.

data.Name: Frightening Injection
data.Short Description: Intimidate your foes when you inject them
data.Benefit: When you successfully inject a creature with a substance (including a biohacker’s biohack) using a weapon that has the injection special property, you can attempt an Intimidate check to demoralize that creature as a move action before the end of your turn. A creature can be affected by this ability only once per day.

data.Name: Frightful Display
data.Prerequisites: Cha 15, Intimidate 5 ranks, ability to cast spells
data.Short Description: Intimidate a foe as a reaction after they fail a save against your spell
data.Benefit: As a reaction when a creature fails a saving throw against a spell you cast this round, you can attempt an Intimidate check to demoralize that creature. You can target only one creature regardless of how many creatures your spell affected, and a given creature can’t be targeted by this ability more than once per day.

data.Name: Grab Attention
data.Short Description: Make a foe you hit in melee off-target when attacking anyone other than you
data.Benefit: When you hit a target with a melee attack, you can attempt an Intimidate check, using the same DC as you would to demoralize that foe. If you succeed, the creature is off-target while attacking anyone other than you until the beginning of your next turn. A creature can be affected by this ability only once per day.

data.Name: Grappler Pull
data.Short Description: Use a grappler to draw your foes closer to you
data.Benefit: When you grapple a creature with a grappler (Core Rulebook 219), you can attempt a reposition combat maneuver against that creature on your next turn as though it were within your reach. If you succeed, you can move the target only directly toward you.

data.Name: Grenade Mastery
data.Short Description: Increase the saving throw DCs of grenades you use
data.Benefit: The save DC of any grenade you use is increased by 1. If the grenade has an item level 5 or more lower than your base attack bonus, you instead increase the DC by 2.

data.Name: Ground Fighting
data.Short Description: Ignore penalties to unarmed attacks when grappled, pinned, or prone
data.Benefit: The normal penalties to attack rolls from being grappled, pinned, and prone don’t apply to your unarmed attacks. The penalties from the pinned condition don’t apply to your attempts to grapple your opponent or free yourself.

data.Name: Hauler
data.Short Description: You can carry much more than usual
data.Benefit: You treat your Strength score as 3 higher for the purpose of determining your carrying capacity. In addition, when you are encumbered or overburdened, you take only a –4 penalty to Strength- and Dexterity-based checks.

data.Name: Improved Energy Resistance
data.Prerequisites: Con 13, character level 10th, energy resistance from a racial trait
data.Short Description: Your natural energy resistance is more pronounced
data.Special: You can take this feat multiple times—its effects don’t stack. Each time you take this feat, it applies to a different energy resistance from a racial trait.
data.Benefit: Your resistance to one type of energy damage from a racial trait stacks with one other source of energy resistance. If it already stacked, that resistance increases by 5 instead.

data.Name: Instant Crater
data.Short Description: Weapons with the explode special property create difficult terrain
data.Benefit: As a standard action, you can make a ranged attack using a weapon that has the explode weapon special property, targeting an intersection as normal and choosing one square adjacent to that intersection. If you hit the targeted intersection and your damage roll exceeds the hardness of the material of the chosen square, that square becomes difficult terrain. This is in addition to the normal effects of the weapon.

data.Name: Jet Charge
data.Short Description: Boost your charge attacks with jets to move farther
data.Benefit: You can activate a jetpack or jump jets armor upgrade (or similar equipment) as part of a charge action. If you do, you can move up to three times the fly speed normally granted by that equipment.

data.Name: Laugh at Danger
data.Short Description: Grant an ally a +2 morale bonus to AC as a reaction when you take Hit Point damage
data.Benefit: As a reaction when you take Hit Point damage from a significant enemy (Core Rulebook 242) that doesn’t reduce you to 0 Hit Points, you can grant allies within 30 feet a +2 morale bonus to AC until the end of your next turn. This is a sense-dependent effect. Once you’ve used this ability, you can’t use it again until you take a 10-minute rest to regain Stamina.

data.Name: Living Ladder
data.Short Description: Hit an adjacent foe with an unarmed attack to stand and possibly knock them prone
data.Benefit: When you’re prone and hit a target with an unarmed attack, you can stand without using an action. If the target of your unarmed attack is your size or smaller and your attack roll result exceeds their AC by 5 or more, that target falls prone.

data.Name: Lung Puncture
data.Short Description: Your critical hits with piercing weapons cause foes to be fatigued
data.Benefit: When you score a critical hit with a weapon that deals piercing damage, in addition to any other critical hit effect you would apply, you also impose the fatigued condition on the target for 1d4 rounds.

data.Name: Melt Defenses
data.Short Description: On a critical hit, cause further attacks against a foe to target EAC
data.Benefit: When you score a critical hit with an attack that deals acid damage, instead of applying the normal critical hit effect, you can disrupt the target’s defenses for 1d4 rounds. Attacks against a creature whose defenses have been disrupted in this manner target EAC, regardless of the attacks’ damage type.

data.Name: Memory Access
data.Prerequisites: Construct type, constructed racial trait, exocortex, or head slot augmentation
data.Short Description: +2 insight bonus to skill checks to identify creatures and recall knowledge
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 insight bonus to skill checks to identify creatures and recall knowledge.

data.Name: Minor Eldritch Lore
data.Prerequisites: Key ability score 15, caster level 4th, 4 levels in a class with a class spell list
data.Short Description: Gain an additional 0-level spell known
data.Special: For every 3 levels by which your caster level exceeds 4th, you can take this feat an additional time. Each time you select this feat, you gain a different 0-level spell from your class spell list.
data.Benefit: You gain one additional 0-level spell from your class spell list.

data.Name: Lesser Eldritch Lore
data.Prerequisites: Key ability score 17, Minor Eldritch Lore, caster level 7th, 7 levels in a class with a class spell list
data.Short Description: Gain an additional 1st-level spell known
data.Special: For every 3 levels by which your caster level exceeds 7th, you can take this feat an additional time. You cannot take this feat more times than you have taken Minor Eldritch Lore. Each time you select this feat, you gain a different 1st-level spell known from your class spell list.
data.Benefit: You gain one additional 1st-level spell known from your class spell list.

data.Name: Eldritch Lore
data.Prerequisites: Key ability score 19, Feat Lesser Eldritch Lore, Feat Minor Eldritch Lore, caster level 10th, 10 levels in a class with a class spell list
data.Short Description: Gain an additional 2nd-level spell known
data.Special: For every 3 levels by which your caster level exceeds 10th, you can take this feat an additional time. You cannot take this feat more times than you have taken Lesser Eldritch Lore. Each time you select this feat, you gain a different 2nd-level spell known from your class spell list.
data.Benefit: You gain one additional 2nd-level spell known from your class spell list.

data.Name: Greater Eldritch Lore
data.Prerequisites: Key ability score 21, Feat Eldritch Lore, Feat Lesser Eldritch Lore, Feat Minor Eldritch Lore, caster level 13th, 13 levels in a class with a class spell list
data.Short Description: Gain an additional 3rd-level spell known
data.Special: For every 3 levels by which your caster level exceeds 13th, you can take this feat an additional time. You cannot take this feat more times than you have taken Eldritch Lore. Each time you select this feat, you gain a different 3rd-level spell known from your class spell list.
data.Benefit: You gain one additional 3rd-level spell known from your class spell list.

data.Name: Major Eldritch Lore
data.Prerequisites: Key ability score 23, Feat Eldritch Lore, Feat Greater Eldritch Lore, Feat Lesser Eldritch Lore, Feat Minor Eldritch Lore, caster level 16th, 16 levels in a class with a class spell list
data.Short Description: Gain an additional 4th-level spell known
data.Benefit: You gain one additional 4th-level spell known from your class spell list.

data.Name: Momentous Attack
data.Short Description: On a melee critical hit, move 5 feet
data.Benefit: When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon against an adjacent enemy, you can immediately move 5 feet to any square adjacent to both you and your target. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity. This effect is in addition to any other critical hit effects.

data.Name: Oracular Gift
data.Short Description: Gain +1 to initiative checks, and you can cast an enhanced augury once per day without spending RP
data.Benefit: You gain a +1 bonus to Perception checks to act in the surprise round, as well as to initiative checks. Additionally, you can cast augury once per day without spending a Resolve Point to do so, and add your caster level to the percentage chance of the spell giving you a meaningful reply.

data.Name: Percussive Maintenance
data.Short Description: Reroll checks for engineer crew actions in starship combat and engineering checks to repair items
data.Benefit: Once per day, you can reroll an Engineering check to repair an item. In addition, you can reroll one engineer crew action per starship combat.

data.Name: Pinpoint Hurler
data.Short Description: Reduce the distance your thrown weapons travel when they miss
data.Benefit: If you miss on a ranged attack with a thrown weapon (Core Rulebook 245), you can reduce the result of the d4 roll to determine how many squares away the weapon lands by 1.

data.Name: Poison Adaptation
data.Short Description: Gain a +2 enhancement bonus to saves against poison and take half damage from initial exposure
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 enhancement bonus to saving throws against poison, and you take half damage from initial exposure to poisons.

data.Name: Poison Rejection
data.Short Description: Recover from poisons more swiftly
data.Benefit: When you fulfill a poison’s cure condition (or reach the end of its duration), you move one step toward healthy. You also move one step toward healthy for each night of normal rest; this can be increased as normal using the long-term care task of Medicine.

data.Name: Positive Conduit
data.Short Description: Gain extra Hit Points from magical healing
data.Benefit: When you regain Hit Points from a magical effect or serum, you regain an additional amount of Hit Points equal to your Constitution modifier. Once you use this ability, you can’t use it again until you take a 10-minute rest to regain Stamina Points.

data.Name: Protective Fur
data.Short Description: Gain +2 circumstance bonus to saves against heat and cold dangers and +4 circumstance bonus to avoid contracting contact afflictions
data.Special: At the GM’s discretion, other furred races might meet the prerequisites for this feat.
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves against cold and heat dangers. In addition, you gain a +4 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves made to avoid contracting contact diseases and poisons.

data.Name: Quicker Trickler
data.Short Description: Administer serums as a standard action instead of a full action
data.Benefit: You can trickle serums down the throats of other creatures as a standard action instead of as a full action.

data.Name: Reality Glimmer
data.Prerequisites: Cha 15, character level 5th, no levels in witchwarper
data.Short Description: Gain the ability to cast minor witchwarper spells
data.Benefit: Choose two 0-level witchwarper spells and one 1st-level witchwarper spell. You can cast the 0-level spells at will and the 1st-level spell once per day for every 3 character levels you have. For these spells, your caster level is equal to your character level and the key ability score is Charisma. If you later gain levels in witchwarper, you lose the benefits of this feat and can replace it with either Spell Focus or Spell Penetration.

data.Name: Reject Chains
data.Short Description: +2 insight bonus to saves against charm and compulsion effects, and you can reroll saves against such effects
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 insight bonus to saving throws against charm and compulsion effects, and once per day you can reroll a save against such an effect.

data.Name: Ricochet Grenade
data.Prerequisites: Proficiency with grenades, base attack bonus +7
data.Short Description: Bounce a grenade off a wall to reach difficult spots
data.Benefit: As a standard action, you can make a ranged attack with a grenade, targeting an intersection that’s adjacent to a wall or large object. If you hit, you can pick a direction for the grenade to travel and then roll 1d4 to determine how far from the targeted intersection the grenade lands. The direction must be away from the wall or object, and not parallel to or through it. If you miss, use the normal rules for missing with a thrown weapon (Core Rulebook 245).

data.Name: Sacred Strike
data.Prerequisites: Worship a deity of an alignment within one step of your own
data.Short Description: Treat your attacks as having your deity’s alignment
data.Special: If you change which deity you worship, this feat gives you no benefit until you next gain a character level, and then only if you still meet the prerequisite.
data.Benefit: When you make an attack as a standard action against a creature, your attack is treated as having the same alignment as the deity you worship for the purposes of overcoming damage reduction.

data.Name: Shelter Ally
data.Prerequisites: Feat Bodyguard, natural reach of 10 feet or more
data.Short Description: Use Bodyguard to protect allies within reach and increase the bonus and penalty by 1 when protecting a smaller creature
data.Benefit: You can use Bodyguard to protect an ally within your natural reach, rather than just an adjacent ally. The bonus and penalty to AC from the Bodyguard feat are each increased by 1 when you use the ability on a smaller ally.

data.Name: Shield Block
data.Short Description: Spend 1 Resolve Point as a reaction to reduce damage from area effects
data.Benefit: As a reaction when you would take damage from an effect that allows a Reflex save, you can spend a Resolve Point to reduce the damage you take from that effect by an amount equal to your shield’s item level.

data.Name: Shield Proficiency
data.Short Description: Gain proficiency with shields
data.Special: Mechanics with an exocortex, solarians, soldiers, and vanguards gain proficiency with shields automatically at 1st level.
data.Benefit: You gain proficiency with shields. See page 124 for more on shields.

data.Name: Slam Down
data.Short Description: Your critical hits with bludgeoning weapons knock down your foes
data.Benefit: When you score a critical hit with a weapon that deals bludgeoning damage, in addition to any other critical hit effect you would apply, you also apply the knockdown critical hit effect. If the attack would already apply a knockdown critical hit effect, you can choose to also move the target 1d4×5 feet directly away from you.

data.Name: Solid Stance
data.Short Description: Become harder to move, and use a reaction to avoid being knocked prone
data.Benefit: Once per combat when an attack or effect would knock you prone, you can ignore that effect as a reaction. In addition, the distance that any nonmagical effect that would move you against your will is reduced by 5 feet.

data.Name: Soothing Telepathy
data.Short Description: +2 insight bonus to saves against mind-affecting effects that you can extend to nearby allies
data.Benefit: You gain a +2 insight bonus to saving throws against mind-affecting effects. Once per day as a reaction, you can grant allies within range of your telepathic communication a +2 insight bonus to saving throws against mind-affecting effects until the end of your next turn.

data.Name: Spell Feint
data.Short Description: When you successfully feint, your foe takes a –1 penalty to saves against your next spell
data.Benefit: When you succeed at a feint combat maneuver, instead of the normal effects, your opponent takes a –1 penalty to saving throws against the next spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

data.Name: Spellguard
data.Short Description: Grant an ally a +2 insight bonus against a spell as a reaction
data.Benefit: As a reaction when an adjacent ally is targeted by a spell or spell-like ability, you can create a minor protective ward, granting that ally a +2 insight bonus to their saving throw against that effect. This effect distracts you from your own defenses, and you take a –2 penalty to saving throws against spells and spell-like abilities until the beginning of your next turn. Once you use this ability, you can’t use it again until you regain Stamina Points with a 10-minute rest.

data.Name: Tactful Advisor
data.Short Description: Provide an additional +1 enhancement bonus when your aid another check result is 20 or higher
data.Benefit: When you aid another, if the result of your check is 20 or higher, you provide an additional +1 enhancement bonus to your ally’s skill check.

data.Name: Tailored Serum
data.Short Description: Grant additional Hit Points to a particular creature with a customized serum of healing
data.Benefit: With 1 minute of work, you can modify a serum of healing so that when it’s consumed by or administered to a specific creature, it restores a number of additional Hit Points equal to the serum’s item level. You can modify a number of serums this way each day equal to your Intelligence modifier. These modifications expire after 24 hours, returning the serum to its normal function.
